subject: ela
description: Acquire and use general, academic, and specialized vocabulary words and phrases in a variety of settings.
elements: a. Acquire general, academic, and specialized vocabulary words and phrases through grade-level texts and content. (I)
b. Use grade-level general, academic, and specialized vocabulary words and phrases to communicate in a variety of settings. (C)

subject: ela
description: Acquire and apply word analysis skills to deconstruct and construct words to make meaning.
elements: a. Identify and use frequently occurring root words and affixes (e.g., prefixes, suffixes) to deconstruct words to determine or clarify meaning. (I)
c. Construct words using frequently occurring root words and affixes. (C)

subject: ela
description: Make connections between words and phrases and use reference materials to determine or clarify word meanings in a variety of settings and for a variety of purposes.
elements: a. Use context within and beyond a sentence to determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases. (I)
b. Determine the relationship between words and their synonyms and antonyms. (I)
c. Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs that differ in manner (e.g., look, peek, glance, stare, glare, scowl) and adjectives that differ in intensity (e.g., large, gigantic). (I)
d. Use glossaries and beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases. (I)
e. Use knowledge of word relationships and learned vocabulary words and phrases when making word choices in speaking and writing. (C)

subject: ela
description: Acquire and use general, academic, and specialized vocabulary words and phrases with precision in a variety of settings.
elements: a. Acquire a range of general, academic, and specialized vocabulary words and phrases through grade-level texts and content. (I)
b. Use grade-level general, academic, and specialized vocabulary words and phrases to enhance communication in a variety of settings. (C)

subject: ela
description: Acquire and apply word analysis skills to determine the meanings of words and to communicate with precision in a variety of settings.
elements: a. Deconstruct words using common roots, root words, and affixes to determine meaning. (I)
b. Identify affixes associated with specific parts of speech (e.g., *ly* is commonly used with adverbs) to aid comprehension. (I)
c. Construct words using common roots, root words, and affixes. (C)

subject: ela
description: Make connections between words and phrases and use reference materials to determine or clarify word meanings in a variety of settings and for a variety of purposes.
elements: a. Use context (e.g., images and graphs) within and beyond a sentence to determine or clarify the meaning of unknown words and multiple-meaning words and phrases. (I)
b. Identify homophones and homographs and use the relationship between synonyms and antonyms to clarify word meanings. (I)
c. Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs (e.g., toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (e.g., thin, slender, skinny, scrawny). (I)
d. Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to check spelling and to determine or clarify the precise meaning of words and phrases. (I)
e. Use knowledge of word relationships and learned vocabulary words and phrases to make precise word choices when speaking and writing. (C)

subject: ela
description: Acquire and use general, academic, and specialized vocabulary words and phrases with precision in a variety of settings.
elements: a. Acquire a range of general, academic, and specialized vocabulary words and phrases through grade-level texts and content. (I)
b. Use grade-level general, academic, and specialized vocabulary words and phrases to enhance communication in a variety of settings. (C)

subject: ela
description: Acquire and apply word analysis skills to determine the meanings of words and to communicate with precision in a variety of settings.
elements: a. Deconstruct words using knowledge of Greek and Latin roots, root words, and affixes to determine meaning. (I)
b. Explain how the part of speech and the meaning of a root or root word changes based on the use of different affixes (e.g., *beautiful* as an adjective vs. *beautifully* as an adverb). (I)
c. Construct words using knowledge of Greek and Latin roots, root words, and affixes. (C)

subject: ela
description: Make connections between words and phrases and use reference materials to determine or clarify word meanings in a variety of settings and for a variety of purposes.
elements: a. Use context (e.g., definitions, examples, or restatements in text) to determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple- meaning words and phrases. (I)
b. Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their synonyms, antonyms, homophones, and homographs. (I)
c. Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related adverbs (e.g., quickly, swiftly, rapidly). (I)
d. Use print and digital reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses) to check spelling and determine or clarify the precise meaning of words and phrases. (I)
e. Use knowledge of word relationships and learned vocabulary words and phrases to make precise word choices when speaking and writing. (C)

subject: ela
description: Acquire and use general, academic, and specialized vocabulary words and phrases with precision in a variety of settings.
elements: a. Acquire a range of general, academic, and specialized vocabulary words and phrases through grade-level texts and content. (I)
b. Use grade-level general, academic, and specialized vocabulary words and phrases to enhance communication in a variety of settings. (C)

subject: ela
description: Acquire and apply word analysis skills to determine the meanings of words and to communicate with precision in a variety of settings.
elements: a. Deconstruct words using knowledge of Greek and Latin roots, root words, and affixes to determine meaning. (I)
b. Explain the connection between roots, root words, affixes, and parts of speech to determine the meaning of words (e.g., *discuss* as a verb vs. *discussion* as a noun). (I)
c. Construct words using knowledge of Greek and Latin roots, root words, and affixes. (C)

subject: ela
description: Make connections between words and phrases and use reference materials to determine or clarify word meanings in a variety of settings and for a variety of purposes.
elements: a. Use context (e.g., cause/effect relationships and comparisons in text) to determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases. (I)
b. Identify analogies and use synonyms, antonyms, homophones, and homographs to clarify intended meaning. (I)
c. Distinguish shades of meaning among related words, including verbs, adjectives, and/or adverbs, to clarify intended meaning. (I)
d. Use print and digital reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses) to check spelling and determine or clarify the precise meaning of words and phrases. (I)
e. Use knowledge of word relationships and learned vocabulary words and phrases to make precise word choices when speaking and writing. (C)

subject: ela
description: Use a variety of approaches to continuously build vocabulary across a range of real-life, academic, and disciplinary contexts and apply those understandings to analyze texts and to communicate effectively.
elements: a. Acquire a range of general, academic, and disciplinary vocabulary through grade-level print, digital, and/or multimodal texts or content. (I)
b. Use grade-level general, academic, and disciplinary vocabulary to communicate clearly and precisely in a variety of settings. (C)

subject: ela
description: Use word knowledge and word analysis skills to determine the meaning of unfamiliar words and phrases and to communicate effectively for a variety of purposes.
elements: a. Deconstruct words using etymology knowledge, Greek and Latin roots, root words, and/or affixes to determine or clarify meaning in grade-level texts. (I)
b. Apply knowledge of parts of speech to determine the meanings of words and phrases in grade-level texts. (I)
c. Construct words based on knowledge of Greek and Latin roots, root words, and/or affixes and use those words appropriately in context. (C)
d. Use knowledge of parts of speech to determine precise words and phrases when constructing texts. (C)

subject: ela
description: Analyze the denotative and connotative meanings of words and phrases and strategically apply those understandings when interpreting and constructing texts.
elements: a. This progression transitions to 6-8.L.V.3.b.

b. Analyze relationships between words, phrases, and/or clauses (e.g., synonyms, antonyms, analogies, contextual clues) to determine, distinguish, or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words and phrases. (I)
c. Distinguish between the connotations of words that share a similar denotation (e.g., confident, assertive, egotistic, pompous, smug). (I)

d. Use available print and/or digital resources, including reference materials and digital tools (e.g., online search, embedded word processing features), to determine, clarify, or verify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words and phrases. (I)
e. Determine or clarify the nuanced meanings of closely related words or phrases using available print and/or digital resources to make strategic decisions when speaking and writing. (C)

elements: a. Acquire a range of general, academic, and disciplinary vocabulary through grade-level print, digital, and/or multimodal texts or content. (I)
b. Use grade-level general, academic, and disciplinary vocabulary to communicate clearly and precisely in a variety of settings. (C)

b. Apply knowledge of parts of speech to determine the meanings of words and phrases in grade-level texts. (I)
c. Construct words based on knowledge of Greek and Latin roots, root words, and/or affixes and use those words appropriately in context. (C)
d. Use knowledge of parts of speech to determine precise words and phrases when constructing texts. (C)
